item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It seems like this object would be effective if ever there were an emergency requiring the rapid recruitment of new personnel within the Foundation.

Some sort of viral meme slipped past the filters & wiped out the Helpdesk? That's ok, just flash 2140 at your friendly local Geek Squad, no biggie.

+1 for implications.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This article was really great, and was enhanced greatly by the crosslinks. Things were a bit foggy at first, but clicked into place with a little jumping around. And I got to read more than one great new article! Yay.

This article, both my most controversial and my personal favorite, achieves another milestone.

Edit:. Having originally finished 29th, it is now in 6th place!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The original instance that was found at the archeological site and keyed to some ancient cult.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: *likes your post*

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What is SCP-2140-1-D?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That's adorable.

I think that's one of the best things about this community. Every once in a while some talented person surprises me with unsolicited legitimately professional illustration of my work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not sure you've seen this but I saw cutesy(?) fanart of this on teh intrawebs, here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Mind fucking blown.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Or maybe it couldn't make us 2/2140 employees because the Foundation doesn't exist in our universe. So instead of creating one in our world, the path of least resistance to getting us access to this document was to retro-causally create a wiki-based fiction writing project about a fictional Foundation, who's articles just happen to exactly match the documentation of the real Foundation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Anomalies will be as anomalies will be.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The entire Thaumiel object class consists of scips which are useful.
There are plenty of others that are useful without being Thaumiel, as well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why not?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: SCPs shouldn't be useful.

item.title: Re: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Here are the shitty hand drawn and MSPaint versions by me that were the basis:
http://scpsandbox2.wikidot.com/local--files/sirpudding/Glyph.jpg
http://scpsandbox2.wikidot.com/local--files/sirpudding/Glyph.gif
There are symbols here that are supposed to be the black sun, the hanged king, the yellow sign, and antlers for the scarlet king or DEER.

item.title: Re: Author Post
item.content:encoded: It's not a secret code- the references are pictographic. Also, collapse your images in the forums.

item.title: Re: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
I decoded as many of the glyphs as I could, but I don't know what to do with the black circles or any of the letters decoded. :/

+ Show image
- hide

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
How does the foundation realize what the picture does if ' only the foundation ' have seen it technically. if the 'tests never existed' wouldn't they just keep running tests until they had a shitload of employees? at what point did the retro-causality become apparent

Inductive logic. We know that a bunch of things here don't make sense. The events involving the original instance, the fact that a secret project to make a Foundation version existed. The fact that no D-Class has ever been tested with this. These things don't follow logically from the current timeline. They are explicable if this retroactively alters the past and if you postulate a lost timeline where the Foundation had to do Kallinkos to reconvert people after the original Daeva version almost took over the Foundation. However there is no direct evidence of this, nor can there be.

I don't think a couple coincidences would convince people that their entire life has been a lie. Some 05 has to remember sending in that "covert foundation team" due to retro-casuality all of their memories ret-conned seconds ago? Wouldn't the simple solution be that they had always been a foundation employee?

Yes, that is the case. However it makes the least number of changes possible. So if you have like a Yale guy who was recruited out of the CIA and you have a guy who doesn't have a college degree who was recruited because they saw some weird stuff at the Circle K (which caused them to not go through with robbing it) one of these is not like the other.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ive +1d, because I love the basic Idea, but im running into some logical errors.

How does the foundation realize what the picture does if ' only the foundation ' have seen it technically. if the 'tests never existed' wouldn't they just keep running tests until they had a shitload of employees? at what point did the retro-causality become apparent? especially due to the nature of retro-causality it should never be noticeable. Am I totally missing something?

I don't think a couple coincidences would convince people that their entire life has been a lie. Some 05 has to remember sending in that "covert foundation team" due to retro-casuality all of their memories ret-conned seconds ago? Wouldn't the simple solution be that they had always been a foundation employee?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: None of the links have changed content, I have only added hyperlinks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
For what it's worth, I preferred this without the links to 140, but then there isn't enough in the article to get that, so no problem.

I really like this. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes it can be changed. just PM the kind of phone you have if you still have this issue and wish to change it, i know how for most phones, and wish not to disrupt your privacy. If this is no longer relevant, then just comment here saying so please.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: More crosslinks! Yay!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Added some crosslinks!

This, my most controversial article, has slowly climbed from the lowest rated of my works to my third highest. I am pleased and surprised. I wonder how it is rated relative to other short works contest entries now?

ETA: It is now #14 in the contest; up from finishing 29th!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
All right, this means my plans fall close to your own take on this.

You are already on my short list of people who I trust to use my stuff with minimal supervision. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: All right, this means my plans fall close to your own take on this. You'll know it when you see it, but it may be a while.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't really have any opinion of what Nobody is. I liked the story where Nobody is something that field operatives made up to cover for their screw ups, and I have been trying to figure out how to write about Nobody as Odysseus, but other than that I got nothing.

If Nobody is real and immune, I suggest doing something like I suggested for Able; strongly archetypical entities are somewhat resistant, because their natures override their loyalties. Able can be loyal and still kill everyone on his team because killing people, even on his side, is what Able does. Maybe Nobody can be loyal to the Foundation's goals and still do whatever the hell Nobody does.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Considering dropping a casual, subdued reference to this in an SCP but would like clarification from the author: do you think Nobody (as in, the recurring site non-character) would be unaffected by variations of this or would they be converted as well?

Alternatively, would it be an acceptable interpretation/use to write Nobody as inherently immune to this?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Relevant

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Either auto-correct or old-fashioned typo, I guess. I am imperfect.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As a an adult? I'm trying to think about how that error could be made.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What's the path of least resistance? What makes the least number of changes?

I think it is either a) the person is born eariler in the new timeline, or b) the person becomes a Foundation employee as an adult.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What happens if a juvenile views it? Does the object reconstruct time so that they will become a Researcher viewing it in the future, or does it retroconvert them into a Foundation Personnel?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: An AK-Class Subjective Reality Restructuring Scenario. It obviously is a highly illogical world where everyone belongs to the same secret conspiracy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well that's an AK-Class Scenario where everyone in the world is a loyal Foundation employee.

Would that be an AK-Class "New-World-Order" scenario?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So the way this works for 2140 is that the Foundation has a set of comprehensive background information and demographics on a large number of people, both Foundation employees and civilians, even some cooperative defectors from other GOIs, but none with 2140 clearance. They did a lot of stastical analysis and figured out some baseline similarities that high clearance the Foundation people in common have relative to the others. Everyone with 2140 clearance is tested against this baseline, and the more they deviate from this the more likely that they were retroconverted, but having a high degree of similarity to the baseline (what BASILISK personnel call "SigCon" for "significant consensus") doesn't mean that you weren't retroconverted (just that you were already the kind of person that might have joined the Foundation anyway).

So with Armand's poster, you might suspect retroconversion if you are otherwise a vegan with lactose intolerance, whey allergies and celiac disease, who still likes queasdillas despite needing lactase pills and epinephrine shots and suffering painful cramps and vomiting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well that's an AK-Class Scenario where everyone in the world is a loyal Foundation employee.

It probably, by the way, doesn't require that everyone be exposed, just that some critical mass are exposed that tips the path of least resistance to "everyone just works for the Foundation to begin with" being more likely than "this one unlikely person was recruited by the Foundation under some improbable circumstances", how many people that is probably impossible to predict, so that is why the Foundation is really careful about deploying this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Both of those questions are answered in the article:

A) Inductive reasoning. The odds of a)the archeological team all being Daeva cultists b) the Eta-10 cell and c) probably hundreds of additional traitors that were retroconverted during the outbreak (who were retroconverted back after 2140 was created; in fact this is the the reason 2140 was necessary in the first place) are astronomically small. This is confirmed by the fact that no person exists who has seen 2140 that lacks clearance.

B) Project Kallinikos; the glyph was analyzed and reconstructed piecemeal. As a final precaution against -1-D infection everyone who worked on Kallinikos was exposed to the final version of 2140 and then amnesticized.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've been thinking about writing a tale where the Foundation makes everyone in the world see it, in order to end anomalous phenomena for good. How do you think such an event would work out?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I always loved this article for the implications alone, it gives so much room for expansion, but the contest severly limits this.

Quick nitpick: If this thing changes history everywhere to give the person the path of least resistance, how did the Foundation:

A) Figure out the anomaly, as even memories would be affected by the history warp

B) Reverse engineer -D without causing high retrocausilties.

Other than that, i like it, but it could definitively be improved and expanded on. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Difficult or impossible to determine.

More later…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So, I read that, and that same evening I made quesadillas for dinner. Have I been affected?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How did I miss that?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Turns out GAW uses one that makes you like quesadillas

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love the concept of retrocausality, and I think it was handled well. I got most of it on my first readthrough, and the Daevite tag helped clarify it without making it too obvious.

Now, people keep talking about CI using this, but what about other GoI's? I can picture Are We Cool Yet? making one of these with the McDonald's logo and posting it at a Foundation site. Or Gamers Against Weed making one with the Blizzard logo that can only be viewed be people who have a Lvl. 60 WoW character.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It is really neither of those things.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You made the SCP logo into an SCP not write a treatise on inductive reasoning. I was going to not vote but having to read through all of your comments looking for explanation and/or context has made me decide to downvote.

item.title: Of relevance
item.content:encoded: http://papermenplasticmonster.blogspot.com/2016/10/blog-or-treat-scp-2140-retroconverter.html?m=1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Don't feel too bad, this was very deliberately challenging to understand because I set it up so that it requires inductive reasoning, and unlike deduction we don't actually get much practice with induction.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks guys! Man I feel dumb now :(

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The only people who have ever actually seen the object have had 2/2140 clearance in and been perfectly loyal to the Foundation - but they might not have had that clearance or even been Foundation employees before viewing the image.

SCP-2140 works by rewriting the history of the viewer - and of basically EVERYTHING IN THE UNIVERSE that person has ever interacted with, affected, or been affected by - so that the viewer has ALWAYS been a perfectly loyal Foundation employee with 2/2140 clearance, no matter if they were originally a janitor, a double agent for the Chaos Insurgency, a bunch of insane cultists, or a hiker that took a wrong turn somewhere.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It retroactively turns people into loyal Foundation personnel with clearance to view it. Like, if you see it, you turn into someone who is supposed to see it, and everyone and everything changes to make it seem like it's always been that way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't understand? I'm guessing that this is some sort of object that makes you loyal to the foundation, but I don't understand what it is or how it does that. It's probably just me being dumb but could someone explain it please?

item.title: Re: First Impression:
item.content:encoded:
Incidentally, I didn't mean "arbitrary" in the sense of seemingly random or nonsensical, but as being up to the decision of a final authority.

I agree about the Foundation, but I headcanon Thaumiels. :)

item.title: Re: First Impression:
item.content:encoded: Yeah, I suppose I am generally of the opinion that The Foundation isn't suicidally obsessed with ideological purity. I like a ruthlessly pragmatic Foundation.

item.title: Re: First Impression:
item.content:encoded:
Even in a no-Thaumiel universe, most of these steps would have been necessary anyway. See-No-Evil and independent research teams like Project Kallinikos are the only safe way to research this anomaly, and research must be conducted 1) to understand the anomaly sufficiently to effect containment, 2) to understand the connection between the anomaly and the Daeva, and 3) to understand the anomaly in general.

The decision to create SCP-2140 from 2140-D by the O5s was arbitrary and the reasons for it are not provided, but as the last paragraph implies, there are many reasons it's practical. It proves the research teams correctly understand the implementation (if not the full mechanics) while also providing a means for ensuring that none of the research teams got the full picture and are now secret cultists. If it works, it makes it a nice little self-cleaning loop and gives you a countermeasure for future containment.

In a non-Thaumiel universe, they'd probably have done all the research they could without creating -2140, and then just shot all the Kalinikos personnel and closed the files. Then SCP-2140-D would be it's own super-Keter-class object, with the containment procedures of "Keep the fragments hidden and pray we never see this again", no countermeasures.

item.title: Re: First Impression:
item.content:encoded:
the Foundation is both capable of and willing to reverse-engineer SCP-objects and create new ones arbitrarily,

Just because they were able to reverse engineer 2140-1-D doesn't logically mean they can reverse engineer anything. This was a glyph that rewrites history, and could be rewritten slightly so that it works a little bit differently. This is a pretty special case.

What's wrong with just terminating the turned agents the old fashioned way, for example?

They did. The three Eta-10 traitors were killed, if they had been rewritten they wouldn't be in the article.

That said it is pretty obvious why a living loyal employee is more useful than a dead traitor, I would think it is self evident in fact.

I don't understand why the Foundation chose to do it.

Are you objecting to the concept of Thaumiel class objects in general? Yeah if you aren't going to "headcanon" Thaumiel at all, this one is also not a thing.

Otherwise the Foundation chose to do it primarily in order to counteract other 2140-1-D instances should any be discovered, and secondarily as a hard counter to any effects that alter personal history or control minds.

item.title: Re: First Impression:
item.content:encoded:
No, I just meant that the way you used it to overwrite all those cultists' histories makes it sound like the Foundation is both capable of and willing to reverse-engineer SCP-objects and create new ones arbitrarily, whenever it seems like a convenient solution to a problem. (What's wrong with just terminating the turned agents the old fashioned way, for example?)

I understand how it works. I don't understand why the Foundation chose to do it.

item.title: Re: First Impression:
item.content:encoded:
Why not expose everybody to it when they first sign up?

There are ethical reasons to not willy nilly alter personal histories without permission. There is also a major safety issue with overuse, since the "path of least resistance" hypothesis is poorly supported and understood, the Foundation isn't sure if, or when, a certain number of exposures hits a critical population that shifts the world into one where everyone is a Foundation employee to begin with or similar AK scenarios.

Also you run the risk of losing whatever you recruited them for, and you won't even know it. Imagine if you are trying to recruit a wizard for the Bibliographers, 2140 might just turn them into someone who never developed magical ability in the first place.

That said, maybe the Foundation does do this. Most employees aren't cleared for Thaumiel, so they would be amnesticized after exposure. This doesn't prevent personnel from being turned later though.

Why not expose turncoats to it as soon as you identify them, instantly retconning their breaches or other indiscretions into loyal actions?

Yes, this one is probably done, and is definitely hinted at here. This isn't perfect, because you still need to find the mole and take them into custody. It also won't necessarily plug the leak rather then just shifting it.

Why isn't there one on the wall in every Foundation break room, right next to the coffee maker? Why isn't every secure Site wallpapered with it?

Because it is a Thaumiel object and most Foundation employees probably aren't even cleared to know about specific Safe class anomolies? Because then anybody could just copy it and break reality? Because it might hit criticality and break reality if you did that? Because it might leak, get copied and break reality?

Breaking reality is probably bad.

And most of all, if the Foundation is willing to create and deploy this thing, why not utilize a hundred other skips in a thousand different ways?

Are there a hundred skips which can be directly used to counteract or contain other anomolies? Do you have any specific examples?
